notes: Self-correction iteration for Gemini CLI: within ONE user turn the agent calls run_shell_command `false` (fails), inspects the failing functionResponse, retries with run_shell_command `echo recovered`, then replies 'done'. The whole arc is a SINGLE contiguous working span — no flicker to ready or waiting between the failed attempt and the recovery. WHY no flicker (parser.go + tailer.go): the failing tool output is a user-role functionResponse routed to function_call_output WITHOUT ClearToolNames so it never settles (parser.go:133-140); the error/cancelled tool status sets IsError only, which the classifier explicitly ignores (tailer.go:991-992); the recovery assistant message carries toolCalls so it stays assistant_message, NOT turn_done (parser.go:209-211). State settles to ready only on the final text-only 'done' message (non-empty content, zero toolCalls → turn_done). The driver runs `gemini -y` so neither shell call trips a permission gate → never waiting. intermediate_working pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 2000 — long enough to span both retry cycles given typical Gemini latency; if the parser/classifier ever surfaced a failing tool_result (or an intermediate text-only message between the two shell calls) as a transition to ready or waiting, this phase fires. PROVISIONAL on emission: a doc/code read can prove the parser won't flicker on a tool result, but cannot prove Gemini never writes an intermediate text-only message BETWEEN the failing and recovery shell calls (which would settle early then reopen) — only the live recording promotes this from provisional to settled. observations assert the widened verify vector: a Google model id, non-zero cost and tokens, agent=gemini-cli.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after gemini launch
User prompt drives ready → working; the failing run_shell_command `false` and its corrective `echo recovered` retry both run inside this span (spec bullet 1)

State stays continuously working across all retry cycles — the failing `false` functionResponse must NOT flicker the session to ready or waiting before the `echo recovered` recovery cycle starts (spec bullet 2)
After the recovery succeeds the agent replies 'done' (final text, zero tool calls) → working → ready; state never entered waiting (spec bullet 3)
